severity.critical: 12 impact categories incl. network shutdown, chain split, fund freeze, proof verification bypass
severity.high: 2 impact categories incl. transaction delays >500% of average block time

addresses.name: AggregateVerifier
addresses.notes: Top-level multiproof aggregator combining TEE + ZK paths. ~850 LoC. Permissionless proof submission — solhunt strong zone is access-control which this lacks. Bug surface is timing/threshold/IAnchorStateRegistry interaction, EIP-2935 blockhash history, proof nullification. Outside solhunt's benchmark distribution.

addresses.name: TEEVerifier
addresses.notes: Validates AWS Nitro attestations + 65-byte signatures. Crypto-heavy, weak fit for solhunt's pattern detection.

addresses.name: NitroEnclaveVerifier
addresses.notes: AWS Nitro Enclave attestation document parser. CBOR-decoding + cert-chain validation territory. Solhunt has zero benchmark coverage here.

addresses.name: TEEProverRegistryProxy
addresses.notes: Proxy. Implementation is teeProverRegistryImpl. Treat the impl as the real surface.

addresses.name: TEEProverRegistryImpl
addresses.notes: BEST FIT FOR SOLHUNT in this program. Owner/manager-gated registerSigner/deregisterSigner/setProposer/setGameType. Access-control is solhunt's 75% strong zone. Should be the single first scan if/when chainId+RPC plumbing is patched.

addresses.name: DelayedWETHProxy
addresses.notes: Bond/withdrawal contract. Proxy. Impl is delayedWETHImpl.

addresses.name: DelayedWETHImpl
addresses.notes: Standard Optimism delayed-withdrawal pattern. Solhunt has limited coverage of this flavor; could surface a re-entrancy bug if one exists.

addresses.name: OptimismPortal2Impl
addresses.notes: OP Stack portal implementation. CAUTION: scope explicitly excludes 'OP Stack components' but the deployed proxy is in scope as part of the Azul activation. Treat as borderline — only the Azul-specific changes count.

addresses.name: DisputeGameFactoryImpl
addresses.notes: Factory for dispute games. Standard OP Stack pattern. Scope ambiguity same as OptimismPortal2Impl.

addresses.name: AnchorStateRegistryImpl
addresses.notes: Tracks canonical L2 state roots. Cross-protocol state surface — solhunt's weakest zone.

fitAssessment.reasoning: Base Azul is the L2 network upgrade itself (TEE + ZK dual proof system). The scope is dominated by AWS Nitro attestation parsing, ZK proof verification, dispute-game timing/bonds, and cross-protocol state oracles. Solhunt's strong zones are reentrancy (83%), access-control (75%), logic-error (60%) — application-layer DeFi. The Azul surface lives in zones solhunt scored 50-57% on (price-manip, flash-loan) PLUS adds entirely-out-of-distribution patterns (TEE/ZK crypto). Only TEEProverRegistryImpl is a clean access-control fit.
blockers: src/index.ts:272 hardcodes chainId=1 in fetchContractSource — Base Sepolia (84532) sources cannot be fetched without a code patch.
ETH_RPC_URL env var is Ethereum mainnet — Base Sepolia anvil fork requires a Base Sepolia RPC. The --chain CLI flag is just a label and does not change the RPC endpoint.
fitAssessment.recommendation: DO NOT auto-scan. Patch chainId+RPC plumbing on a branch first, then run a single targeted scan against TEEProverRegistryImpl (0xF9Ab...0D849) only. Treat as a stretch attempt; queue Drips Network and other higher-fit Immunefi programs as primary use of remaining Max budget.
